cards.back: A type of equilibrium in a single population where players randomize their strategies
A concept in game theory where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged
A type of equilibrium where no player randomizes their strategy, instead choosing a fixed action
A strategy where a player assigns a probability to each possible action, rather than choosing a single action

A type of equilibrium where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged, and assuming that the randomization is perfect
